name: Phosphor Motes
description: The window sheds embers of luminous dust from its own frame. Each mote is born on an edge, mostly the top one, peels away along it and then curls upward through the clear margin, trailing its own curved path history. Near motes are larger, brighter and faster than far ones, a few flare into rare bright embers, and a fine halo of twinkling micro dust hugs the frame. Colour burns through the brand gradient over each mote's life, born cyan at the frame and fading out rose at the end of its climb. The dust dims when the surface loses focus.
author: PlasmaZones

parameters.name: Emanation reach
parameters.description: How far from the frame the dust travels before burning out (px)

parameters.name: Mote count
parameters.description: How many motes are in the air at once

parameters.name: Mote size
parameters.description: Radius of each mote's glowing head (px)

parameters.name: Tail length
parameters.description: Length of the fading comet tail (px)

parameters.name: Sway
parameters.description: How far each mote drifts sideways as it rises (px)

parameters.name: Micro dust
parameters.description: Density of the fine twinkling dust between the motes, 0 to disable
